title: The Accounting Equation
event: Reyes Co. reports total assets of $250,000 and total liabilities of $90,000. Compute total equity.

computationPaths.narration: The equation never breaks: Assets = Liabilities + Equity. Solve for whichever piece is missing.

memorize.body: Assets = Liabilities + Equity — every transaction keeps this balanced.
Two of the three are always given. Rearrange: Equity = Assets − Liabilities; Liabilities = Assets − Equity.

distractors.feedback: You added instead of subtracting. Equity is what's LEFT of assets after creditors' claims: 250,000 − 90,000.
That's the liabilities. Equity is the residual: assets minus liabilities = 160,000.
